column1: The cabbie snarls a "Where to, buddy?" as he swings the meter flag down.
column2: 計程車司機惡狠狠地說：「去哪兒，老兄？」同時按下計程表。

column1: Lookin' for action, eh? Dis town is full of it. We gotta disco that's usually full of foxes this time of night. Den dere's the casino, next to the all-night weddin' chapel. And we gotta lovely bar, but you've been dere. Me, I like da convenience store next to da disco, but of course, that's just personal preference.
column2: 「尋求刺激是吧？這鎮上多得是。咱們有間舞廳，這鐘點通常美女滿滿。然後啊，還有賭場，就在全天候禮拜堂隔壁。咱還有間不錯的酒吧，你去過啦。我個人嘛，比較喜歡舞廳旁的便利店，當然啦，這只是個人偏好。」

column1: It won't take us long to get back dere, but I'll have to circle 'round da block.
column2: 「回那邊不會花太久，不過我得繞過街區。」

column1: Well, here we are, buddy. That'll be $%v200.00.
column2: 「好啦，到了老兄。該給 $%v200.00。」

column1: So, wadda we gonna do, buddy? Ya just gonna sit here all night? I gotta earn a livin' too, ya know!
column2: 「那怎樣啦，老兄？你要在這坐整晚嗎？我也得賺口飯吃啊！」

column1: Why don'cha wait'll we git there, buddy?
column2: 「為啥不等咱們到那兒再說，老兄？」

column1: Sorry, but the meter's runnin'. Where ja wanna go?
column2: 「抱歉，計程表在跑。你要去哪兒？」

column1: Thanks, buddy. Maybe I'll see ya again sometime!
column2: 「謝了，老兄。也許哪天還會見面！」

column1: Waddaya tryin' to do, peahead! You're not gonna stiff me, and get away with it!
column2: 「你在搞什麼呢，傻瓜！別想賴帳溜掉！」

column1: You wish you hadn't. Mr. America, he ain't. His strongest feature seems to be his odor!
column2: 你會後悔看他。美國先生他不是。他最突出的特徵好像是他的臭味！

column1: He wonders why you keep looking at him, and flashes a coy little smile back at you in the mirror. Your stomach turns, and not from the ride!
column2: 他納悶你為啥一直看他，在鏡子裡回給你個嬌羞的微笑。你的胃翻江倒海，不是暈車！

column1: The driver looks at you and says, "Hey! What's that you got? Wine?" He grabs your bottle of wine, and guzzles it down!
column2: 司機看著你說：「嘿！你拿的啥？紅酒？」他一把搶過你的酒瓶，猛灌下肚！

column1: Ok, but it'll cost ja extra!
column2: 「好吧，不過要加錢！」

column1: Waddaya mean, 'no money!!' snarls the driver. "Step outside my office, chump!"
column2: 「什麼叫『沒錢！！』」司機怒吼。「滾出我的車，傻瓜！」

column1: Ok. You got it, Mac!
column2: 「好啦，沒問題，老兄！」

column1: Hang on, Larry. He's swerving all over the road!
column2: 抓好，Larry。他在路上大搖大擺！

column1: Whew! You're glad to get out of here.
column2: 呼！你很高興終於逃出來了。

column1: It seems a bridge abutment has brought your game to a sudden stop! Remember, Larry: "Friends don't let cabbies drive drunk!"
column2: 好像橋墩讓你的遊戲戛然而止了！記住啊 Larry：「真朋友不會讓計程車司機酒駕！」

column1: I'm sorry, but it's against my personal philosophy to accept a gratuity!
column2: 「抱歉，不過收小費違背我個人哲學！」

column1: Sheepishly, you apologize to the cabbie for not carrying enough money to pay for the ride.
column2: 你尷尬地向計程車司機道歉，因為沒帶足夠的錢付車資。

column1: So? Are ya gonna get outta my cab, or not?? Thanks for da business, but I didn't want ta make no career outta this!
column2: 「怎樣？你要滾出我的車不？謝謝你的生意，不過我不想靠這個當飯碗！」

column1: So? Are ya gonna pay up, or do I have ta get rough with cha??
column2: 「怎樣？你要付錢，還是要我動粗？」

column1: It seems to be running a little fast, but then, this is an expensive town!
column2: 好像跑得有點快，不過嘛，這是個高消費的城鎮！

column1: You wonder if cab companies have full-time employees to make these floors sticky.
column2: 你納悶計程車公司是否有專職員工讓地板黏黏的。

column1: Even the ceiling's grungy.
column2: 連天花板都髒兮兮的。

column1: Ok, buddy, says the cabbie. "But why would cha wanna go there?"
column2: 「好啦，老兄，」司機說。「不過你幹嘛想去那兒？」

column1: He sure drives fast, doesn't he?
column2: 他開得真快，不是嗎？

column1: Nope. Next time, make up your mind before ya gets in da cab!
column2: 「不行。下次上車前先決定好！」

column1: You are outside Lefty's bar. Isn't it wonderful what can be done with a little neon?
column2: 你在 Lefty 酒吧外。霓虹燈的魔力真妙不可言，不是嗎？

column1: There is a sign on that utility pole.
column2: 那根電線桿上有個招牌。

column1: It appears that the hooker gave you a little more than you bargained for!
column2: 看起來那個妓女給你的比預期多得多！

column1: Considering the looks of this place, you decide that next time you'd be better off to spend a little more money on your computer games!
column2: 看這地方的樣子，你決定下次花多點錢在電玩上會比較划算！

column1: While life may be possible, it is no longer worth living.
column2: 雖然還能活著，但人生已不值得活。

column1: The windows are too dirty to see through.
column2: 窗戶太髒了，根本透不了光。

column1: Nah. Lefty would probably break both your legs!
column2: 別啦。Lefty 大概會砸斷你兩條腿！

column1: Nice tuck and roll job, eh?
column2: 不錯的翻滾停靠啊，嗯？

column1: Welcome
column2: Welcome

column1: Amazed by your own cleverness, you lift the door mat and discover...
column2: 為自己的聰慧驚喜，你抬起門墊，發現……

column1: ...absolutely nothing!
column2: ……根本沒有東西！

column1: (What did you expect? A key?)
column2: （你期望啥？鑰匙？）

column1: You can't. The door is securely locked.
column2: 不行。門牢牢鎖著。

column1: Lefty's closes at 3:00 a.m.
column2: Lefty 的營業時間到凌晨 3 點。

column1: STOP, PERVERT!!! shouts the cop.\n\n"I wonder who he's yelling at," you think.
column2: 「住手，色鬼！！！」警察大喊。\n\n「我不知道他在吼誰，」你想。

column1: Caught you, pervert! the cop shouts. "We don't like people exposing themselves around this town, especially wearing one of those!"
column2: 「抓到你了，色鬼！」警察喝道。「我們這鎮上不喜歡有人亂露，尤其穿著那種東西！」

column1: Next time, if you insist on wearing your "lubber" in public, you should at least zip your fly.
column2: 下次，你堅持在公開場合穿著那種「乳膠褲」，至少要拉上拉鍊。

column1: He appears to be chasing someone!
column2: 他好像在追什麼人！

column1: Try moving to the east end of the fire escape.
column2: 試試移動到逃生梯的東邊。

column1: Try moving to the west end of the fire escape.
column2: 試試移動到逃生梯的西邊。

column1: %m7 and a fence.
column2: %m7 和一道柵欄。

column1: Nah. That room needs the fresh air!
column2: 算了。那房間需要透透氣！

column1: You are on a fire escape. A rusty ladder is at one end. East of the fire escape is another window, just out of your reach.
column2: 你站在逃生梯上。一端有條生鏽的梯子。逃生梯東邊是另一扇窗，剛好搆不著。

column1: %m7, a fence, and something sitting in the east window.
column2: %m7、一道柵欄，還有東邊窗裡坐著的東西。

column1: It's a nice little alley, with a trash bin, two windows, a fire escape
column2: 這是條不錯的小巷，有垃圾桶、兩扇窗、一台逃生梯

column1: Move closer to the ladder.
column2: 靠近梯子。

column1: (Of course, you can't play the violin now, but you get the idea.)
column2: （當然啦，你現在拉不了小提琴，但你懂意思。）

column1: Unlock it? From the outside?
column2: 開鎖？從外面？

column1: You can't. It's locked from the inside.
column2: 不行。它從裡面上了鎖。

column1: Looking inside the west window, you see the room you just left.
column2: 看著西邊窗戶內，你看到剛才離開的房間。

column1: Looking at the east window, you notice a bottle of pills sitting on the window ledge.
column2: 看著東邊窗戶，你注意到窗台上放著瓶藥丸。

column1: There are two windows high above you. The west window by the fire escape has a dim, reddish glow; the east window is dark. There may be something on the sill of the east window, but you can't make it out from down here.
column2: 上面遠處有兩扇窗。逃生梯旁的西邊窗發著暗紅色微光；東邊窗一片黑暗。東邊窗台上可能有東西，但下面看不清楚。

column1: You broke it, didn't you?
column2: 你打破它了，不是嗎？

column1: You lean far out over the railing before remembering that agility is yet another of your short suits.
column2: 你身體傾出欄杆，才想起靈敏度也是你的短處。

column1: With the rope securely holding you to the railing, you can reach all the way over to the window. NOW, of all times, you notice the window's locked!
column2: 繩子把你牢牢綁在欄杆上，你可以伸過去碰到窗戶。現在、偏偏現在，你才發現窗子鎖著！

column1: Subtly, you smash the window with your hammer. Your future as a second-story man seems quite plausible!
column2: 小心翼翼，你用榔頭砸碎窗戶。你當竊賊的前景真是光輝璀璨！

column1: You got 'em! But what are they?
column2: 你搞定了！可是那是啥？

column1: You might try reaching over to the window first.
column2: 你可以先試試伸手去那扇窗。

column1: You consider sticking your hand through a solid glass window, but realize that if you did, you wouldn't be able to play the violin.
column2: 你考慮把手穿過玻璃窗，但想到這樣的話你就拉不了小提琴了。

column1: It already is.
column2: 它已經是了。

column1: What is this; a death wish?
column2: 這是啥；死願嗎？

column1: You already took them. Why do this again?
column2: 你已經拿了。為啥又要來一遍？

column1: Amidst assorted, odoriferous refuse from Lefty's clientel, you discover Lefty's old, left-handed hammer.
column2: 在 Lefty 客人的各種臭烘烘垃圾中，你發現了 Lefty 的老舊左手榔頭。

column1: There's nothing else here worth describing, let alone taking.
column2: 這裡沒別的東西值得一提，更別說拿了。

column1: YUCK!!
Unfortunately, you can't see much from out here.
column2: 可惜的是，你從這兒看不了太多東西。

column1: Wow! Does your head hurt!!
column2: 哇！你的頭好痛！！

column1: Hey! Where are you?
column2: 嘿！你在哪兒？

column1: After a careful analysis of your surroundings, you conclude you are at the bottom of a pile of garbage!
column2: 經過仔細分析周遭，你結論是你在一堆垃圾的底部！

column1: Move to the front of the trash bin.
column2: 移動到垃圾桶前面。

column1: There's no way to reach the ladder from down here. It's only accessible from above.
column2: 從下面沒辦法碰到梯子。只能從上面進去。

column1: You see alleys stretching off to infinity.
column2: 你看見小巷一直延伸到無窮遠。

column1: The fence is much too tall for you to climb over.
column2: 柵欄對你來說太高了，爬不過去。

column1: It's not long enough to reach from here.
column2: 它長度不夠，搆不到這裡。

column1: It's not long enough to reach anywhere, but you might want to use it for safety purposes.
column2: 它長度根本不夠搆到任何地方，不過你可以拿它當安全用途。

column1: Be more specific.
column2: 說具體點。

column1: You untie the rope.
column2: 你解開繩子。

column1: Using the dull pocket knife, you cut the rope free.
column2: 用鈍鈍的小刀，你割開繩子。

column1: A rickety fire escape, complete with safety ladder, is loosely attached to the building. A mild, reddish glow comes from the window behind it.
column2: 一台搖搖晃晃的逃生梯，配著安全梯子，鬆散地附在大樓上。窗戶後面發著淡紅色微光。

column1: Unfortunately the lid on the trash bin just will not close.
column2: 可惜垃圾桶的蓋子就是蓋不上。

column1: Try as you might, it is impossible to move the trash bin.
column2: 怎麼試都沒用，垃圾桶根本動不了。

column1: The solution is closer to you than that.
column2: 解法比你想的更近。

column1: There is an old neon sign hanging on the wall that says "Hotel"...
column2: 牆上掛著一個舊霓虹招牌，寫著「Hotel」……

column1: ...approximately.
column2: ……大概啦。

column1: You can't. Even its rusty bolts are too tough for you!
column2: 不行。就連它生鏽的螺栓都太硬了！

column1: Not now!
column2: 現在不行！

column1: There may be something in the east window, but from here, you're not sure.
column2: 東邊窗可能有東西，不過從這兒看你不確定。

column1: It's a long way to the ground!
column2: 到地面的距離好遠！

column1: Move closer to the window, if you want to go back in the hooker's room.
column2: 靠近窗戶，如果你想回到那妓女的房間。

column1: You reconsider after noticing the trash bin is behind where you would fall!
column2: 你注意到垃圾桶在你會摔下去的地方後面，於是放棄了。

column1: You are in Lefty's one and only restroom. The stench is overpowering, there is graffiti on the walls, and you doubt that the sink was clean even when it was installed. Even roaches can't survive in this place!
column2: 你在 Lefty 唯一的洗手間。臭到爆炸，牆上全是塗鴉，你懷疑這水池裝上去時都沒乾淨過。連蟑螂都活不了。

column1: %m8Scott me up, Beamie!"
column2: %m8Scott me up, Beamie!"

column1: Ahhhhhhh.
column2: 啊啊啊啊啊。

column1: %m8Attention arcade game players: please don't eat the urinal cakes!"
column2: %m8Attention arcade game players: please don't eat the urinal cakes!"

column1: %m8It takes leather balls to play rugby!"
column2: %m8It takes leather balls to play rugby!"

column1: Funny. No one knocks back.
column2: 好笑。沒人敲回來。

column1: NOW??
column2: 現在？？

column1: You see many messages. One is:\n\n"
column2: 你看到許多訊息。其中一個是：\n\n"

column1: You see a handsome, dashing, macho, sexy, young man. (Obviously, this mirror has quite a bit of distortion!)
column2: 你看到一個英俊、瀟灑、陽剛、性感、年輕的男人。（很明顯，這鏡子有不少扭曲！）

column1: You grab an ancient copy of the "Ball Street Journal," and settle in to "sit and think!"
column2: 你抓起一份古老的《Ball Street Journal》，打算「坐著思考！」

column1: You feel a great relief!
column2: 你感到極大的解脫！

column1: Why, look! Some woman removed her diamond ring to wash her hands, and forgot to take it with her.
column2: 咦，看啊！某個女人脫下鑽戒洗手，卻忘了帶走。

column1: Got it!\n\nYou briefly consider an attempt to find the ring's owner, but return to your normal self and pocket it instead.
column2: 搞定！\n\n你一度考慮要找到那戒指的主人，但馬上回神，改為偷偷塞進口袋。

column1: A small, neatly lettered sign reminds you:\n\n"Since we can't clean up after every customer, please rinse out this sink when you are finished."
column2: 一個小招牌提醒你：\n\n「因為我們沒法在每個客人後都清潔，請在用完後沖洗水池。」

column1: Evidently, you are the first customer who can read.
column2: 顯然地，你是第一個會讀字的客人。

column1: My, my. Aren't we the little sick-o!
column2: 哎呀呀。我們真是個小變態啊！

column1: Say, here's an interesting one:\n\n"The password is: 'Ken sent me.'"
column2: 嘿，這個滿有趣的：\n\n「密碼是：『肯叫我來的』」

column1: Bad idea, eh Larry?
column2: 不是好主意，對吧 Larry？

column1: Your life passes before your eyes.
column2: 你的人生在眼前閃過。

column1: You doze briefly.
column2: 你短暫地打了個盹。

column1: What's that aroma?
column2: 那是什麼香味？
